author: @reinhardtonk1510
text: I think it's a witty thing. Thanks

author: @Patsgardenlife
text: Very interesting video. Thanks for sharing.👍🏾

author: @rogerbarton497
text: Nice one.  If nothing else it can be used to demonstrate the relationship between torque, power and speed and the effect of gears and pulleys.  BTW, the spindle motor is the one that turns the CD, CDs' floppies, Hard disks, exchangeable disks are all mounted on the spindle.  I'd call the motor that moves the head the "head actuator".

author: @cecilcooper6210
text: And with a boost converter, you might in a survival mode be able to get your phone up to call for help.

author: @Draakdarkmaster6
text: you could probably just get a bigger more powerful motor and use that as your drive motor, then hook up something like this to a stationary exercise bike.

author: @jamest.5001
text: I have a old cordless drill from like 1990, I was thinking about making a charger for USB stuffs, maybe use a couple super caps, and DC converters, crank it until it gets easy to crank, then plug in the phone or what ever, then crank a bit more occasionally, basically just Chuck up a crank, and connect wires directly to the motor, it's a little 9.6v drill, so surely I can get 5-6v from it, if not a DC converter will fix that while  regulating the voltage,  living off grid , things like this comes in handy, although a hand crank air conditioner isn't happening! Ha..   Great video,

author: @fizzyelectricity
text: Have you heard of the Lockridge device ? ? Found during the clean up in Berlin after ww2, by a soldier who took it back to the States,  it was basically a modified dynamo from a car, with a capacitor added to give it a 3rd phase, and it was tuned to it's electrical load.

author: @macanalista
text: I already made it, added a LED and voilà, have a handle lantern!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate

author: @Covey1969
text: Absolutely love your videos and how your mind works. What if you were to connect it to a USB battery charger, could you hand turn a charge for the batteries to eventually charge a phone in an emergency? For Example...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate and for sure

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: Thanks for that, I saw a vid where someone used the whole CD tray mechanism to generate energy, so he only had to slide the disc tray in and out. Not that it could be used for anything, I just liked the idea of using the CD tray’s movement to spin the motor.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: this is basically the same thing mate - all you would do is leave the tray attached solder on two wires and bingo

author: @tebbi67
text: oh yeah i forgot, the most voltage i measured was with stepper motors. . . they went up to 60 volts. I had removed it from an old inkjet printer.

replies.author: @Kiyarose3999
replies.text: There’s a v small but powerful motor inside Microwave cookers, it’s the ( Synchronous) motor that turns the glass plate inside the cooker. They produce 240v as soon as you turn the spindle, unfortunately the Wattage is low, they vary from 2-4W.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: maybe I should do something on a stepper motor - they are really easy to convert

never ending great ideas.......the handcrank is very simple but good.
THX for your ideas...and videos!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: If you can get a somewhat bigger stepper motor, say from an old laser printer, it's a pretty decent generator on it's own wo gears and stuff. Depending on type ( usually 4 or 6 wires ) some diodes could rectify the output and provide enough to charge a phone for example.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: steppers are a really easy conversion mate

author: @karlmyers6518
text:
Hey Rob. Been so busy trying to build my new business from your inspiration and ideas I seem to have little time left for the investigation side of things. Is the a conductive ink I can use on an heating element.  I've brought some 1000c exhaust paste and was thinking about mixing in graphite.  I could do with someone's opinion/ wisdom on this matter. 
Not to blow smoke up your butt but you sir have truly changed my life. Thankyou for being you and doing.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are an awesome guy mate - the main thing you have is the work you are putting in - in my mind that never fails to bring reward - you can add sodium silicate tot he ink - this will help with heat resistance but any carbon based ink is going to start burning off over 400 degrees C - so it will heat but not run a furnace unless you want to recoat every now and then

replies.author: @poosmate
replies.text: Good luck mate!

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: Thank you, very interesting and informative. Great to see something being repurposed.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Glad you enjoyed it

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: Attach a candle powered Stirling engine to the flywheel and add a Bluetooth speaker and you'll have an equivalent to the bayliss but with light and no spring winding!:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one mate - cheers

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Stepper motors with a flywheel and a handle give better results, they can be rigged with 5 volt regulators and charge batteries similar to a dynamo.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they do mate - and I hate to say this but repurposing the old CD and introducing ideas of gearing was the point not getting better results on the generation

author: @pulesjet
text: Tie it to a Hamsters exercise wheel.  Make em pay it's own light bills.  LOL

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: lmao, this would be a perfect motor to attach to the hamster wheel. Might have to take off a cog or two but you will generate power.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hang a peanut in front of them and with enough you could power a house lol

replies.author: @rastamanralph6670
replies.text: Put a cream cracker in front of it and it will give you 12 v😂👍 mine used to love crackers, it was funny watching it stuff a bit in in its cheeks and get stuck getting into its house and having to manoeuvre it sideways through the hole and kick it out it's mouth with some difficulty,then stash it😂👍🐯

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Good one. I'm surprised that you can actually get that much current out of that particular motor.  I think you've also keyed onto what your wind generators need -- gearing.  Gears should also allow driving more than one generator from a single energy source -- at that cost, size and weight, you could gang those motors up in parallel for a pretty good output.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Great point!

author: @ciaransherry6021
text: Kids bike up on stand. Clip dynamo to back wheel. Phone holder with USB jack on handlebars. Tell kids they can only charge their phones with exercise juice. 😊

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol

replies.author: @Flashahol
replies.text: That is actually a great idea! Alternately, they could play outside while their phone is charging with the slowest charger available, but no exercise = no charge..

replies.author: @MrGigglesGalore
replies.text: Exercise juice sounds weird

Or if not a cellphone an extra lithium battery that could later charge a cellphone? Thanks for your wonderful content.

author: @gusmartin6053
text: You should look into the 28BYJ-48 step morors. They are small, have a built in gear box, and they're very inexpensive. Just add a crank handle and a rectifier and you have a really nice generator.
